runs.content:
**Investigation idea 1:** Two live Cooking pages are invisible to the master catalog. cooking/chef-david-chang.md (updated 2026-06-21, and already correctly listed in cooking/chefs.md's own table alongside Lucinda Scala Quinn, Nigel Slater, and Anthony Bourdain) and cooking/recipe-suggestions.md (the live weekly-suggestions page, now on its fourth dated batch through week of 2026-07-20) are both absent from wiki/index.md's Cooking section entirely — that section still lists only 3 chefs and 3 individual recipe pages, with no suggestions-index row at all (unlike Books' and Podcasts' own suggestions.md pages, which are indexed). Next step: add both rows to index.md's Cooking table.

**Investigation idea 2:** Lightfin's own adaptation-overview has an open 'AI animation tools feasible?' question that a Lightfin production page has already substantively answered, unlinked. lightfin/adaptation-overview.md's Track 2 (Video) lists an unresolved investigate-todo asking 'AI animation tools feasible? (e.g. Kling, RunwayML, Pika)' — but lightfin/video-prompts-ep101-poseidons-trident.md (created 2026-05-28, via the /kids-video-prompter skill) already is that pipeline in production: full scene-by-scene AI video prompts, a character design bible, negative prompts, and a Midjourney-suffixed thumbnail prompt. The specific video-gen tool is still genuinely open, but the broader 'is this feasible' question has real production evidence Track 2 doesn't reference. Next step: cross-link video-prompts-ep101-poseidons-trident.md from adaptation-overview.md's Track 2 and narrow the open question to just 'which video-gen tool.'

**Cross-domain connection 1:** AI & Tech's own project index doesn't list Lightfin's working AI-video production pipeline as a project. ai-tech/projects.md is scoped as 'All software/app/AI projects' but currently lists exactly one entry (Crypto App). Lightfin's /kids-video-prompter output — video-prompts-ep101-poseidons-trident.md — is a genuine, non-trivial AI tooling exercise that by any reasonable definition belongs in an 'AI projects' index, yet it's invisible from AI & Tech's own catalog of what Fred has actually built with AI.

**Cross-domain connection 2:** Games' 'Crypto Portfolio Tracker' prototype stub may be the same build as AI & Tech's live 'Crypto App' project — tracked in two domains, never cross-checked. memory/index.md's Domain Content Status for Games names 'Crypto Portfolio Tracker' as one of four prototype stubs (never transcribed into games/prototypes.md's table). Meanwhile ai-tech/projects.md lists 'Crypto App' as an active project — 'Crypto market data app,' built with Claude Code + MCP. A crypto market-data app and a 'Crypto Portfolio Tracker' game prototype are close enough in description that they may be the same underlying build, described once as a live AI-tech tool and once as an unbuilt game concept, with no link between the two domain pages either way. Next step: confirm with Fred whether these are the same project.

**Investigation idea 1:** Gladiator (Con Iggulden) has sat in reading-list.md for two weeks with zero downstream page. It was added to books/reading-list.md's Books Read table on 2026-07-03 (Summary Page column: '—'), but it's absent from wiki/index.md's Books section entirely, and the monthly book-summarizer task (runs 1st of each month) won't touch it until 2026-08-01 — a full month during which a book Fred has actually read is effectively invisible to the rest of the wiki. Next step: create books/book-gladiator.md manually now rather than wait for the scheduled run, and add the entry to index.md.

**Investigation idea 2:** Scriptnotes is the first podcast-recommender pick ever aimed at screenwriting craft, and Lightfin's own craft-reference page has no section built to receive it. podcasts/suggestions.md's newest (week of 2026-07-16) entry recommends Scriptnotes explicitly because six months of prior recommendations skipped the Creative/writing domain entirely, and names the Ryan Condal (House of the Dragon) episode as the closest analogue to Lightfin's ongoing serialised-narrative work. But lightfin/inspiration-authors.md — the page purpose-built for mapping craft techniques to Lightfin — only covers prose authors (Silverstein, Pratchett, plus a shortlist of Dahl/Milne/Adams/Rosen); it has no section for screenwriting-specific technique. Next step: after trying the Condal episode, add a 'Screenwriters' section to inspiration-authors.md.

**Cross-domain connection 1:** Books' newest recommendation, Chip War, is a ready-made narrative wrapper around AI & Tech's own unresolved semiconductor-chokepoint research. books/suggestions.md's July 2026 entry for Chip War (Chris Miller) cites it as 'a direct match for his TSMC/NVIDIA/HBM supply-chain research,' but the connection runs deeper: ai-tech/data-tsmc-cowos-2026-06-27.md (CoWoS as a single point of failure) and ai-tech/model-ai-ecosystem-constraint-edges-2026-06-27.md (SK Hynix > TSMC > MSFT > NVIDIA investment ranking) are exactly the material the book's narrative would contextualise — and reading it bears directly on two already-open threads: the still-unbuilt Micron idea page (flagged 2026-07-07) and the still-undiligenced GlobalWafers watchlist candidate (flagged 2026-07-13). None of these pages currently reference the book suggestion.

**Cross-domain connection 2:** Podcasts' newest recommendation, Latent Space, points at AI & Tech research that already exists in more dated, specific form than the recommendation itself describes. podcasts/suggestions.md's entry says the show 'goes deepest on the actual mechanics of inference economics' — but the wiki already has two dated pages doing exactly this: ai-tech/data-ai-compute-token-economics-2026-06-26.md and ai-tech/source-arora-token-pricing-2026-07-09.md (Palo Alto Networks CEO's ~90% token-cost-cut thesis). Neither page is cross-linked from the podcast recommendation; if Fred picks up Latent Space, its episodes are the natural place to test or update those existing figures rather than starting the thread fresh.
